items.title: Sensex jumps 736 pts, Nifty closes above 23,800 on US-Iran peace deal announcement
items.summary: Indian benchmarks rallied with Sensex +736 pts and Nifty closing at 23,853.90 (+0.98%) after the US and Iran finalised a peace deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Realty index outperformed, rising nearly 4%. Pharma and healthcare were the only sectors in the red.
items.rationale: Peace deal removes the geopolitical risk premium that had been suppressing broad markets; lower crude directly benefits India's CAD, inflation, and rate-sensitive sectors.

items.title: Brent crude plunges over 4% to ~$83.75/bbl as US-Iran ink ceasefire agreement
items.summary: Brent crude futures fell $3.58 or 4.10% to $83.75/bbl after the US-Iran preliminary peace pact announced reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. WTI declined 4.72% to $80.87. Crude had been above $124/bbl at the conflict peak.
items.rationale: A sharp crude decline is bearish for upstream E&P (ONGC, OIL) and oil marketing company margins are mixed, but broadly positive for India's import bill and macro.

items.title: Indian rupee surges 40 paise to 94.71 per dollar on crude oil fall, US-Iran deal
items.summary: The Indian rupee gained ~40 paise to close at 94.71, its strongest in five weeks. India's 10-year bond yield dropped to 6.83%, a 12-week low, as the US-Iran peace deal reduced inflation expectations.
items.rationale: Stronger rupee and lower bond yields reduce hedging costs for corporates and signal improving macro stability; rate-sensitives (NBFCs, Realty) stand to benefit from lower yields.

items.title: India's WPI inflation surges to 9.68% in May on Fuel & Power jump of 30.33%
items.summary: India's wholesale price inflation rose to 9.68% in May from 8.26% in April. Fuel and Power category surged 30.33% YoY. Mineral oils rose 49.82%, crude petroleum 61.51% YoY. Manufacturing products rose 7.48%. First reading under the revised WPI 2022-23 base year series.
items.rationale: High WPI signals elevated input cost pressure for manufacturing, which could compress operating margins in auto, cement, chemicals, and FMCG sectors.

items.title: RBI MPC June 2026: Repo rate held at 5.25% for third consecutive meeting, stance neutral
items.summary: RBI MPC unanimously voted to hold repo rate at 5.25% and maintain neutral stance. RBI revised FY27 GDP forecast down to 6.6% from 6.9%, and raised CPI inflation projection to 5.1% from 4.6%, citing West Asia conflict energy risks.
items.rationale: A hold removes near-term cut expectations but the downward GDP revision and upward inflation revision introduce caution on earnings. Rate-sensitives remain range-bound.

items.title: India's GDP grows 7.7% in FY26, fastest in three years; Q4 at 7.8%
items.summary: MoSPI provisional estimates show India's FY26 real GDP grew 7.7%, beating consensus of ~7.5%. Q4 FY26 clocked 7.8% growth. Manufacturing expanded 10.7% for the year. Financial services, real estate and IT grew 10.4%. Trade, hotels, transport grew 11%.
items.rationale: Strong FY26 GDP print validates the India growth story and supports equity valuations for broad-based domestic consumption and investment-cycle plays.

items.title: India's retail inflation rises to 3.93% in May 2026, food-led — below RBI's 4% target
items.summary: India's CPI rose to 3.93% in May from 3.48% in April, driven by food inflation at 4.78%. Core inflation firmed to 3.8%. The print was below Reuters poll forecast of 4.0% and remains under the RBI's 4% midpoint target.
items.rationale: Below-target CPI keeps the door open for future rate cuts if growth slows, but the upward trajectory from 2.75% in January warrants monitoring for second-round energy effects.

items.title: Nifty IT tumbles over 2%; Infosys, HCL Tech, TCS among top drags on AI disruption fears
items.summary: Nifty IT index fell over 2% on June 11, with HCL Tech (-3.5%), Infosys (-2.5%), and TCS (-1.7%) leading declines. AI disruption fears — specifically AI's deflationary impact on legacy outsourcing revenue — drove broad sector selling. Nifty IT has fallen ~27% over the past six months.
items.rationale: Anthropic's Fable5 model launch and TCS Chairman's comment that AI agents could equal human headcount by 2028 intensified fears of ADM revenue deflation, compressing near-term earnings visibility.

items.title: TCS loses over ₹4 lakh crore market cap in FY26; stock down 37% — AI transition in focus
items.summary: TCS has lost ₹4.2 lakh crore in market cap over FY26, the largest single large-cap wealth destruction in India's history, trading at ₹2,148 (down 37%). Infosys fell 24%, HCL Tech 29%, Wipro 20%. AI disruption, weak guidance, and falling enterprise IT spend are key drivers.
items.rationale: Structural AI-led deflation in application development and maintenance revenue stream is repricing the entire India IT sector. Analysts at Nuvama warn of 6-8 more quarters of headwinds before AI services revenue offsets legacy decline.

items.title: Defence Ministry signs ₹1,476 crore deal with BEL for mobile electronic warfare systems
items.summary: The Defence Ministry signed a ₹1,476 crore contract with Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) for five ground-based mobile electronic warfare systems for the Indian Army. The contract falls under the Buy (Indian-IDDM) category with 72% minimum indigenous content, reinforcing the Aatmanirbhar Bharat push.
items.rationale: Order accretion directly boosts BEL's order book and provides multi-year revenue visibility. Defence PSUs with strong order pipelines remain well-positioned amid elevated global defence spending.

items.title: Rajnath Singh reviews HAL's LCA programme; AMCA RFP issued, GE-414 engine negotiations ongoing
items.summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh reviewed HAL's LCA Mk1A programme; 18-24 aircraft expected ready by year-end. AMCA RFP was issued to L&T-BEL, Tata Advanced Systems, and Bharat Forge-BEML consortia. Commercial negotiations for GE-414 engines for AMCA prototype are underway.
items.rationale: AMCA programme execution accelerates indigenisation momentum and adds long-dated order optionality for HAL, BEL, and the L&T defence consortium.

items.title: Defence stocks rise up to 4.5% on US-Iran peace deal buzz; Nifty India Defence Index jumps 2.3%
items.summary: Defence stocks staged a strong recovery on June 12, with the Nifty India Defence Index jumping 2.3%. Paras Defence (+4.4%), GRSE (+3.5%), Data Patterns (+3.5%), Mazagon Dock (+2.5%), HAL (+1.3%), BEL (+1.5%), and BDL (+2%) led gains.
items.rationale: The government's ₹69,700 crore Shipbuilding and Maritime Development package combined with robust defence order pipeline of ₹2.3-2.8 lakh crore supports medium-term sector re-rating.

items.title: Godrej Properties sells homes worth over ₹2,000 crore at Bengaluru project Godrej Vanantara in launch week
items.summary: Godrej Properties sold over 1,000 homes worth ₹2,000 crore at Godrej Vanantara, Bengaluru, in its launch week. The 36-acre project has total estimated revenue potential of ₹3,700 crore. GPL retained its position as India's largest residential developer by booking value for the third consecutive year in FY26, with bookings growing 16% YoY.
items.rationale: Sustained demand for premium residential launches underscores the structural realty upcycle. A declining crude-driven CPI trajectory opens the door for rate cuts that would further boost housing affordability.

items.title: Realty index surges nearly 4% on June 15, led by DLF (+4.67%), Godrej Properties (+4.42%), Prestige Estates (+6.14%)
items.summary: Nifty Realty outperformed all sectors on June 15, surging ~4%, as falling crude prices improved inflation outlook and raised hopes of accommodative monetary policy. DLF, Godrej Properties, Prestige Estates, Oberoi Realty, and Phoenix Mills were top gainers.
items.rationale: Rate cut expectations, structural housing demand, and the US-Iran peace deal's positive macro tailwinds create a strong near-term catalyst for realty stocks.

items.title: Wipro ₹15,000 crore share buyback opens June 11–17; offers 5.72% equity at ₹250/share
items.summary: Wipro launched its ₹15,000 crore share buyback via tender offer at ₹250 per share, representing 5.72% of paid-up equity capital. Record date was June 5, 2026. The buyback is funded from free reserves and totals 60 crore shares.
items.rationale: The buyback provides a near-term price floor for Wipro shares, offering small shareholders an attractive exit opportunity at a premium to market price. Signals management's confidence in balance sheet strength despite sector headwinds.

items.title: HDFC Bank hikes lending rates (MCLR) by up to 10 bps from June 8, 2026
items.summary: HDFC Bank hiked its MCLR (Marginal Cost of Funds-based Lending Rate) by up to 10 basis points across tenors effective June 8, 2026. Base rate, BPLR, FD rates and senior FD rates up to 7% remain unchanged.
items.rationale: An MCLR hike raises yields on floating rate loans, marginally supportive of NIM, but could slightly increase borrower stress in a high-cost environment. Watch for broader banking sector NIM trends.

items.title: DIIs absorb ₹4.16 lakh crore in 2026 despite FII selling of ₹2.7 lakh crore; SIP flows at ₹31,115 crore in April
items.summary: Domestic institutions (mutual funds, LIC, pension funds) net bought ₹4.16 lakh crore in Indian equities between January and early June 2026, far exceeding FII outflows of ₹2.7 lakh crore. Monthly SIP inflows stood at ₹31,115 crore in April, up 18% YoY.
items.rationale: Structural deepening of domestic investor participation reduces India's market sensitivity to FII outflows, providing a sustained demand floor for equities and reducing market volatility.

items.title: India-US to hold fresh trade talks on June 23-24; first tranche of BTA enters final stage
items.summary: India and the US will hold trade talks on June 23-24 to finalise the first tranche of the Bilateral Trade Agreement (BTA). Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agrawal said discussions will focus on market access, non-tariff barriers, and giving final touches to the deal. A USTR team will visit India for negotiations with Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal.
items.rationale: Finalising the first phase of the India-US BTA would reduce tariff uncertainty for exporters, especially IT/pharma/textiles, and signal stronger bilateral trade integration towards $500 billion by 2030.

items.title: India's IIP grows 4.9% in April 2026 under new base year; capital goods surge 16%
items.summary: India's factory output grew 4.9% YoY in April 2026 under the revised IIP series (base year 2022-23). Manufacturing rose 6.2%. Capital goods surged 16% YoY. Consumer durables grew 4.3% and consumer non-durables only 2.8%, signalling that capex-led growth is stronger than consumption recovery.
items.rationale: Capital goods' 16% surge is a positive signal for infrastructure and manufacturing capex plays, but the lagging consumer recovery suggests FMCG and consumer discretionary may face near-term volume pressure.

items.title: India's GST collections rise 3.2% YoY to ₹1.94 lakh crore in May; import-led revenue surge
items.summary: India's gross GST collections rose 3.2% YoY to ₹1.94 lakh crore in May 2026. Net customs GST jumped 19.7% while domestic revenue declined 2.3% YoY. April-May combined gross GST rose 6.2% to ₹4.37 lakh crore.
items.rationale: Moderate GST growth reflects softening domestic consumption even as import-linked collections remain elevated due to higher oil prices. The normalization from April's record is expected and not a cause for alarm.

items.title: Macquarie sees power sector upcycle; sets NTPC target at ₹480 amid demand boom
items.summary: Macquarie upgraded its view on India's power sector, preferring NTPC and JSW Energy, citing a sustained electricity demand boom driven by industrialisation, data centre proliferation, EV adoption, and cooling load expansion. NTPC target set at ₹480.
items.rationale: India's power demand growth, supported by industrial capex and data centres, creates a multi-year capacity expansion cycle. NTPC's diversification into renewables and its strong order book support the re-rating thesis.

items.title: L&T signs ₹18,600 crore MoU with Tamil Nadu covering data centres, electronics, and shipbuilding
items.summary: Larsen & Toubro signed an ₹18,600 crore MoU with the Tamil Nadu government covering three high-growth sectors: data centres, electronics manufacturing, and shipbuilding. The projects are expected to generate over 8,200 jobs.
items.rationale: The multi-sector MoU diversifies L&T's revenue streams into high-margin digital infrastructure (data centres) while leveraging the national shipbuilding push and electronics manufacturing scheme.

items.title: Indian steelmakers to launch multi-year capex cycle; capacity target raised to 300 MT — S&P Global
items.summary: S&P Global reports Indian steelmakers are set to begin a multi-year capex cycle to meet the government's 300 million tonne (MT) capacity target. Safeguard duties (11-12%) on steel imports extended through April 2028, protecting domestic producers from cheaper overseas supply.
items.rationale: Extended safeguard duties combined with a long-term capacity expansion cycle create favourable conditions for domestic steel producers. However, near-term crude-driven cost pressures and weak global demand are headwinds.

items.title: Sun Pharma announces FY26 final dividend of ₹5/share; Dr Reddy's launches first-to-market oncology generic in US
items.summary: Sun Pharmaceutical announced a final FY26 dividend of ₹5/share. Separately, Aurobindo Pharma launched Bosutinib Tablets 400mg (generic Bosulif, oncology) as first-to-market in the US, expanding its specialty pharma portfolio.
items.rationale: Pharma companies continue to build US market share through first-to-file generics in high-value specialty segments. However, a strengthening rupee acts as a headwind for dollar-denominated export revenues.

items.title: India's equity mutual fund inflows fall to 1-year low in May at ₹22,908 crore; volatility blamed
items.summary: Equity mutual fund inflows fell 40% MoM to ₹22,908 crore in May 2026, the lowest in a year, as US-Iran war-driven market volatility and elevated crude oil prices dampened sentiment. Small-cap, mid-cap, and large-cap fund inflows all declined.
items.rationale: A one-year low in equity MF inflows signals weakening retail appetite during high-volatility periods. However, SIP inflows remained structurally solid at ₹31,115 crore in April, providing a floor for systematic investment.

items.title: India US Section 301 proceedings: USTR proposes additional tariffs; public hearings set for July 7
items.source: Press Information Bureau, Government of India
items.summary: The USTR has proposed additional tariffs on India under Section 301 of the US Trade Act of 1974 as part of broader action against 60 countries. India remains engaged in proceedings and is separately negotiating the Bilateral Trade Agreement framework announced February 2026. Public hearings on July 7.
items.rationale: Section 301 tariff threat adds uncertainty for Indian IT services exporters to the US and could raise costs of Indian goods in the American market. Resolution via the BTA negotiations is the key watchpoint.

items.title: Indian auto sector: Govt announces excise duty exemption on high-concentration ethanol blends; BYD to enter India PHEV segment
items.summary: The Indian government announced a central excise duty exemption on petrol blended at 22%, 25%, 27%, and 30% ethanol concentrations. BYD announced entry into India's plug-in hybrid segment with the Seal U. Maruti Suzuki announced price protection for small cars ahead of a planned price hike.
items.rationale: Ethanol policy supports green transition but Chinese EV/PHEV competition intensifies. Declining crude prices ease fuel cost concerns and improve consumer sentiment for vehicle purchases.

items.title: NLC India shares fall 3% on OFS announcement; govt to divest 3% stake for ~₹1,263 crore
items.summary: NLC India shares fell 3% to ₹327 after the government announced an offer for sale (OFS) to divest up to 3% stake in the Navratna PSU, aiming to raise around ₹1,263 crore. This is part of the government's divestment programme.
items.rationale: OFS announcements typically create near-term supply overhang and price pressure as market absorbs additional shares. The divestment discount adds selling pressure on the stock.

items.title: India's FY26 fiscal deficit at 4.4% of GDP, meeting revised estimate; April FY27 deficit at 21.4% of target
items.summary: India's FY26 provisional fiscal deficit came in at 4.4% of GDP, in line with the revised estimate. For FY27, the April deficit has already touched 21.4% of the annual target as revenue receipts fell sharply. The government's full-year FY27 fiscal deficit target remains 4.4% of GDP.
items.rationale: Fiscal discipline in FY26 is positive for macro stability. The April FY27 overshoot is seasonal and expected, but warrants monitoring if revenue recovery in Q2 is below expectations.
